behavior_summary: This script provides a set of simple utility handlers that present prewritten book/letter/spell text to the player. Each named function spawns Book::read with a fixed string literal (converted via str_to_ptr) and the calling object ('this'), captures the spawn result into a temporary, and then suspends until the reader process ends.
There is no branching, menu logic, or flag manipulation — each function's sole gameplay effect is to open a book/reader UI with specific content and wait for the player to finish reading.
notable_control_flow: All functions follow the same linear flow: call str_to_ptr on an embedded literal -> spawn Book::read (using the spawn operator <=>) and store the result in a local (temp/pid) -> suspend the current script until the spawned Book::read process finishes.
No conditional branches, loops, or inter-function control transfers are present in this file; functions are one-shot viewers.
open_questions: Which in-game object(s) or item IDs are bound to each function (e.g., which book instance calls U8P_SGBOOK::func0080 vs. U8P_SGBOOK::func08A3)? (confidence: medium — inferred but not present in this file)

Is process_type 0x0001 used by the engine to register these as immediate event handlers (look/use/read) for book objects, and if so what exact engine event triggers each function? (confidence: medium)
Are there any localization or runtime substitutions applied to the embedded literals before display (e.g., player name tokens)? The literals appear fixed. (confidence: low)

main_function.name: U8P_SGBOOK::func0080
main_function.purpose: Start a Book::read viewer showing the text 'THE SAGA OF BONE CRUSHER' (string literal passed via str_to_ptr) and yield until the spawned reader process completes.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player or actor opens/requests to read the associated book object (inferred from the call to Book::read with a book text literal).
